title: Remote Session Duration Anomalies
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="vpn:session" earliest=-7d
| eval dur_hrs=(end_time-start_time)/3600
| eventstats median(dur_hrs) as med, stdev(dur_hrs) as sd by user
| eval z=if(sd>0, (dur_hrs-med)/sd, 0)
| where abs(z)>3 OR dur_hrs>48 OR dur_hrs<0.01
| table user, dur_hrs, med, z, src
description: Statistical outliers in VPN session length — unusually short (brute probe) or long (unattended tunnel) vs UC-17.3.8 fixed thresholds.
value: Security analysts detect statistically anomalous VPN session durations per user, catching sessions that are unusually long (potential persistent access) or unusually short (connectivity issues or failed logins).
implementation: Requires reliable `start_time`/`end_time`. Tune z-score or use `anomalydetection`.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (`sourcetype=cisco:asa`). Data in `index=vpn`.
• Key fields: `user`, `duration` (session duration in seconds), `action=session_disconnect`, `bytes_in`, `bytes_out`.
• At least 7 days of data to establish per-user baselines.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify duration data:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_disconnect" earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(duration) AND duration>0
| stats avg(duration) as avg_sec by user
| eval avg_hours=round(avg_sec/3600, 1)
| sort - avg_hours
| head 10
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Duration anomaly detection:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_disconnect" earliest=-7d
| eval dur_hrs=duration/3600
| eventstats avg(dur_hrs) as user_avg stdev(dur_hrs) as user_sd by user
| eval zscore=if(user_sd>0, (dur_hrs - user_avg)/user_sd, 0)
| where abs(zscore) > 3
| eval anomaly_type=if(zscore>0, "UNUSUALLY LONG", "UNUSUALLY SHORT")
| stats count values(anomaly_type) as types max(dur_hrs) as max_hrs min(dur_hrs) as min_hrs by user
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Calculates per-user session duration baselines and flags sessions that deviate by more than 3 standard deviations. Unusually long sessions (>3 sigma above average) may indicate a forgotten connection, an automated process, or an attacker maintaining persistence. Unusually short sessions (<3 sigma below average) may indicate tunnel instability (UC-17.2.5) or failed logins.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Check 3 flagged sessions — do the durations make business sense?

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — "Duration anomalies (7d)". Row 2 — Table: user, anomaly type, duration, z-score.
Alert: Daily digest of anomalies.

Runbook:
1. **Unusually long session** — Check if the user is still active. Idle sessions should be terminated per policy.
2. **Unusually short** — May indicate connectivity issues. Correlate with tunnel stability (UC-17.2.5).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Duration field not available** — Calculate from connect/disconnect timestamps: `| transaction user maxspan=24h`.
• **Baseline too narrow** — New users have limited history. Exclude users with fewer than 5 sessions.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when someone's VPN connection lasts an unusually long or short time compared to their normal pattern, because that can signal something is wrong — like a forgotten session or a connection problem.
